meaning: adj.有洞察力的,有远见的,精明的,敏锐的
examples: He was a shrewd, sagacious old man.
A sagacious businessman seldom fails in his business.
"Because I knew he wouldn't", was the Master's simple and sagacious explanation.
The earmarks of his speech were sagacious brevity, theatrical elegance, and understated humor.
Mr Assad, a sagacious fellow, is keeping his options open.
